worries.name: Capability regression
worries.worry: Did I break something that was working? Who's going to find out in production before I do?
worries.worry_surface: Number of callers (components, services, customer flows) that depend on the behavior that could break.
worries.rate_event: Every time shared code changes.
worries.gap_condition_markdown: Expensive in any codebase where components have more than a few callers. Grows with caller count and customer base.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Feature flags / canary deployments
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Regression rolls back within minutes; affected customers limited to canary cohort

scope_shrinking_options.name: Decoupled architecture (no shared state, pure interfaces)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Regression cannot propagate beyond the component's direct consumers

scope_shrinking_options.name: Functional / decoupled style (isolation is the easy default)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Errors are self-contained; cross-component propagation is structurally hard

efficiency_options.name: AI exploratory testing (edge-case tests after change)
AI change-impact analysis (untested behaviors, invariants, negative cases)
Unit tests (ad hoc)
efficiency_options.scope: Tested behaviors; effective level drops with coverage gaps

worries.name: Adaptability reduction in touched code
worries.worry: Is this code going to be a nightmare to change when requirements shift next quarter?
worries.worry_surface: Coupling depth of the module -- count mock dependencies or layers of coupling. Proxy for future change effort.
worries.rate_event: Every time code is added without design consideration.
worries.note: AI defaults to mock-based coupling. It will not reach for Nullables or Hexagonal ports unless the orchestration layer injects these patterns on every invocation.
worries.gap_condition_markdown: Cumulative; invisible until severe. Expensive when a large fraction of the codebase lacks clean test seams.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Nullables pattern
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Dependencies become swappable; coupling count drops

scope_shrinking_options.name: Hexagonal architecture (ports and adapters)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Infrastructure dependencies isolated at the boundary

scope_shrinking_options.name: Functional architecture (pure functions, explicit state)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Each function has zero hidden coupling; worry surface = function signature

scope_shrinking_options.name: Nullables template auto-generated alongside each new class
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Correct structure is the starting point; coupling harder to introduce than avoid

efficiency_options.name: Test recipe workflow (injected at write time, scaffolds correct structure)

worries.name: Consistency violation in touched code
worries.worry: Is this consistent with how everything else works, or am I creating a surprise for the next person?
worries.worry_surface: Number of developers and AI agents who will need to understand this code over its lifetime.
worries.rate_event: Every time code departs from established patterns.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Architecture linters
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Structural violations flagged before merge

scope_shrinking_options.name: Planning tool requiring pattern reference
scope_shrinking_options.effect: No new pattern introduced without acknowledging prior patterns

worries.name: Adaptability regression in other areas (design regression)
worries.worry: Did I choose the wrong abstraction and make future work harder for everyone?
worries.worry_surface: Number of modules using the affected abstraction.
worries.rate_event: Every time a structural change affects a shared abstraction.
worries.note: No known Prevention-level mechanism for design quality in the general case.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Modular architecture with clean seams
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Wrong abstraction stays contained; does not cascade to neighbors

scope_shrinking_options.name: AST refactoring tooling (reversible structural changes)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Wrong abstractions undone in two commands; design decisions are low-stakes

worries.name: Consistency violation (structural breakage)
worries.worry: Did I update every single place that uses this interface, or did I miss some?
worries.worry_surface: Number of direct consumers of the changed interface or type.
worries.rate_event: Every time an interface or type contract changes.
worries.note: Capability regression means the code does something different. Structural breakage means the interface changed and some consumers were not updated. Refactoring tools address both within tool scope.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Narrow, well-defined interfaces
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Fewer consumers per interface; smaller fan-out

scope_shrinking_options.name: Decoupled architecture
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Components interact via narrow contracts; breakage cannot cascade

efficiency_options.name: Language-server reference tracking (find all usages before editing)
efficiency_options.scope: All references within language server scope

efficiency_options.name: Strict type system enforced pre-commit
efficiency_options.scope: All type interactions in covered code

efficiency_options.name: AST refactoring tools (rename/move with full dependency awareness)

worries.name: Adaptability reduction in vigilance mechanisms (test duplication)
worries.worry: When I change this code, am I going to have to fix 40 tests that all break together?
worries.worry_surface: Number of tests sharing setup code or mocking the same dependency.
worries.rate_event: Every time a test is written with mocks or duplicated setup.
worries.gap_condition_markdown: Expensive at high test volume. Grows silently alongside the test suite.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Nullables pattern (eliminates mocks -- the largest duplication source)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Dependencies swappable without test rewrite; duplication source eliminated

scope_shrinking_options.name: Functional architecture (pure functions need no mocks)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Functions have no side effects to mock; worry surface approaches zero

efficiency_options.name: Test recipe tool (prevents structural duplication in new tests)
Test recipe workflow injected at write time

worries.name: Consistency violation (partial rename)
worries.worry: Did I update every callsite, or are some places still using the old name?
worries.worry_surface: Number of callsites not updated. Directly countable.
worries.rate_event: Every rename applied to a shared symbol.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Narrow symbol scope
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Fewer uses = fewer places to update

efficiency_options.name: Language-server reference tracking
efficiency_options.scope: All references within language server scope

worries.name: Documentation-code misalignment
worries.worry: Does our documentation (READMEs, how-to guides, API docs, architecture diagrams, decision records) still describe what the system actually does? When docs and code disagree, which one is right?
worries.worry_surface: Number of documented concepts, behaviors, or structures whose documentation no longer matches reality. Countable by auditing docs against the current system for each documented item.
worries.rate_event: Every change to code or system behavior not reflected in documentation, or every doc update not reflected in code.
worries.note: resolution is bidirectional. Sometimes code evolved correctly and docs need updating; sometimes docs reflect intended design and code needs correcting. The error is the misalignment, not the direction.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Fewer, well-bounded documented items
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Smaller surface of docs to keep in sync

scope_shrinking_options.name: Modular design (narrow responsibilities per documented item)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Docs stay accurate longer; each change affects fewer doc surfaces

efficiency_options.name: Human review of docs vs. code
AI alignment scan (compare docs to code; surface discrepancies)
efficiency_options.scope: Findings become permanent linter rules or workflow gates

efficiency_options.name: Architecture linters (check structural intent vs. code)
Doc update embedded in planning workflow
efficiency_options.scope: All changes that touch a documented item

efficiency_options.name: Automated doc-code alignment tool
efficiency_options.scope: All items covered by the tool

efficiency_options.name: Auto-generated documentation (docs derived from code annotations, tests, or living-doc tools; regenerated on every change)
efficiency_options.scope: All items expressible in generated form -- including API docs, behavior docs from tests, compliance docs from test results, governance reports

worries.name: Domain model corruption
worries.worry: Does this concept match what the businesspeople actually call it, or did I invent terminology that drifts from the domain?
worries.worry_surface: Number of domain concepts in the codebase that diverge from business vocabulary.
worries.rate_event: Every time a new abstraction is introduced without domain validation.
worries.gap_condition_markdown: Invisible until business experts can no longer reason about the product without a developer present.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Domain model documentation (glossary, entity definitions)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Provides reference; reduces unnoticed drift

scope_shrinking_options.name: Domain review embedded in planning workflow
scope_shrinking_options.effect: All new abstractions checked before implementation

efficiency_options.name: Domain expert review of new abstractions
Planning tool requiring domain concept mapping
efficiency_options.scope: All new concepts via the tool

worries.name: Security violation (logs expose sensitive data)
worries.worry: Am I accidentally logging a password, session token, or credit card number?
worries.worry_surface: Number of customers whose sensitive data appears in accessible log streams.
worries.rate_event: Every time observability code touches a sensitive data path.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Structured logging with field-level classification enforcement
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Sensitive fields classified at write time; unclassified fields cannot be logged

efficiency_options.name: Automated PII detection in log output

worries.name: Behavioral inconsistency
worries.worry: Does this behave the same way as the similar feature over there, or will users be confused when the pattern breaks?
worries.worry_surface: Number of user journeys following the pattern that was violated.
worries.rate_event: Every time a user-facing behavior or interaction pattern changes.
worries.gap_condition_markdown: Expensive as user base grows and patterns become entrenched.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Design system (documented patterns)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Reduces unnoticed divergence; not enforced

scope_shrinking_options.name: Design system with enforcement gates
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Pattern violations blocked before shipping

worries.name: Decision inconsistency
worries.worry: Does this architectural decision contradict something we decided six months ago? Are we building on conflicting assumptions?
worries.worry_surface: Number of code areas that make assumptions about the violated decision.
worries.rate_event: Every architectural decision made without consulting prior decisions.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Modular architecture (clean seams)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Inconsistent decisions stay contained; cannot cascade to other modules

scope_shrinking_options.name: Architectural experiments (prove before committing)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Wrong experiments affect only experimental scope

worries.name: Security regression (new security code introduces vulnerability)
worries.worry: Did my auth change open a hole I am not seeing?
worries.worry_surface: Number of customers and data objects accessible through the vulnerability.
worries.rate_event: Every time security controls are modified.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Least-privilege design (narrow scope per control)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Vulnerability scope bounded by control's narrow authority

worries.name: Stale direction in context
worries.worry: After this discovery changed our direction, does the AI still have the old plan in reach? Will it blend pre-pivot assumptions into post-pivot work?
worries.worry_surface: Number of stories, plan items, and context fragments that reference the superseded direction and remain accessible to the AI.
worries.rate_event: Every discovery that causes a meaningful pivot in direction.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Short story batches (few stories in flight per cycle)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Less stale plan to invalidate when a pivot occurs

efficiency_options.name: Re-explain current direction at session start
efficiency_options.scope: Single session; AI may still blend old signals from in-context stories

efficiency_options.name: Delete superseded stories; rewrite remaining stories to reflect new direction; reset context to defined base state
efficiency_options.scope: All work through the plan — AI cannot access prior directions because they no longer exist

worries.name: Ungrounded goals
worries.worry: Are we building the right thing, or did we just assume we know what users want?
worries.worry_surface: Story points committed to goals not grounded in evidence.
worries.rate_event: Every planning cycle where goals are assumed rather than validated.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Short iteration cycles
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Less work committed before grounding event; smaller unvalidated backlog

efficiency_options.name: Planning tool requiring grounding source
efficiency_options.scope: All goals made via the tool

worries.name: Decomposition gaps
worries.worry: Did we think through all the tricky parts, or are there hidden surprises mid-sprint?
worries.worry_surface: Number of unidentified variation points. Proxy: count named unknowns; gaps are the unnamed ones.
worries.rate_event: Every decomposition without structured analysis.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Explicit unknown-surfacing practice
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Forces unknowns to be named; reduces hidden surprise count

efficiency_options.name: Planning tool with required fields (variation points, unknowns, dependencies)

worries.name: Missing or wrong success criteria
worries.worry: How will we know if this is actually done and correct?
worries.worry_surface: Number of work items without explicitly defined success criteria.
worries.rate_event: Every work item started without explicitly defined criteria.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Stakeholder review of criteria before work starts
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Wrong criteria caught before any work is done against them

efficiency_options.name: Planning tool requiring criteria fields
efficiency_options.scope: All work with criteria via the tool

worries.name: Priority errors
worries.worry: Are we working on what matters most right now?
worries.worry_surface: Value units of work done on wrong-priority items.
worries.rate_event: Every task selection without explicit priority criteria.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Small batch sizes
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Less work done on wrong priority before correction

worries.name: Reality disconnect
worries.worry: Is what we built actually solving the problem users have?
worries.worry_surface: Number of users experiencing the diverged behavior. Grows with throughput x time without grounding.
worries.rate_event: Every development cycle without grounding in user feedback.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Short iteration cycles
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Less drift accumulates before grounding event

scope_shrinking_options.name: Feature flags and modular deployment
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Diverged behavior rolled back or pivoted quickly

efficiency_options.name: Outcome-based planning with measurement gates
efficiency_options.scope: All work with defined outcome gates

worries.name: Deployment failure
worries.worry: Did the deployment break something in production? How many users are affected?
worries.worry_surface: Number of customers affected during the failure window.
worries.rate_event: Every deployment without automated verification.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Canary deployments / blue-green + automated rollback
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Failure affects small cohort; rollback restores good state within minutes

worries.name: Configuration drift
worries.worry: Is the environment still what we think it is?
worries.worry_surface: Number of environment differences from desired state. Countable with IaC diff.
worries.rate_event: Every environment change made outside declarative management.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Immutable infrastructure
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Environments cannot drift; they are replaced, not modified

worries.name: Evaluation coverage gap
worries.worry: Does our evaluation system actually catch the problems we care about, or does it have blind spots we haven't noticed?
worries.worry_surface: All outputs produced while a gap exists, weighted by defect rate in the uncovered class. Proxy: output volume x estimated miss rate.
worries.rate_event: Every defect in an uncovered class that occurs while the system lacks coverage for it.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Modular output consumers
scope_shrinking_options.effect: A bad output affects fewer downstream systems

worries.name: Monitoring blind spot
worries.worry: Is there a health dimension we are not measuring that could fail silently for weeks before we notice?
worries.worry_surface: Time-to-detection x rate of problems in the unmonitored dimension. Proxy: number of health dimensions with no active metric.
worries.rate_event: Every health problem that occurs in an unmonitored dimension.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Short feedback loops
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Less drift accumulates before detection

efficiency_options.name: Deterministic monitoring guards (one per class the scout found)

worries.name: Oversight mechanism gap
worries.worry: Does our gate actually stop the bad cases, or does it let them through while we think we are protected?
worries.worry_surface: All bad work that passes through the gap x downstream impact. Count uncovered step types.
worries.rate_event: Every bad step or cycle that passes through an insufficiently specified gate.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Small steps with fast feedback
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Less work builds on a bad step before detection

worries.name: Missing escalation conditions
worries.worry: Did the AI keep going past the point where I should have been consulted, because we forgot to define a halt condition for that situation?
worries.worry_surface: Number of autonomous action categories not covered by halt conditions. Directly countable from the circuit breaker rule set.
worries.rate_event: Every novel situation the AI encounters with no defined halt condition.
worries.gap_condition_markdown: Even one uncaught event can be high-cost. Probabilistic alone is insufficient.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Narrow task scope (AI given small, bounded tasks)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Fewer autonomous decisions per invocation; maximum overshoot is small

efficiency_options.name: Deterministic halt conditions (circuit breakers)
efficiency_options.scope: All conditions in the rule set

worries.name: Process enforcement gap
worries.worry: Does our process enforcement actually cover the decisions that matter, or are important rules only documented and not enforced?
worries.worry_surface: Number of decision types or workflow steps without enforcement gates. Directly countable from the workflow definition.
worries.rate_event: Every workflow execution where a required gate is absent.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Modular process (each step independently gated)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: A bypassed step cannot silently affect subsequent steps

worries.name: Scope enforcement gap
worries.worry: Is the scope we defined for each AI agent actually enforced, or do agents have more access than they should?
worries.worry_surface: Files or systems touched beyond authorized scope. Countable from access logs or git diff.
worries.rate_event: Every autonomous action in an insufficiently bounded system.
worries.gap_condition_markdown: No Probabilistic or Deterministic intermediate exists -- either tools enforce it or they don't.

scope_shrinking_options.name: Narrow task scope per invocation
scope_shrinking_options.effect: Possible overshoot surface is small by construction

scope_shrinking_options.name: Tooling restrictions (file-type, read-only access)
scope_shrinking_options.effect: AI cannot act outside scope regardless of intent

efficiency_options.name: Written boundary documentation
efficiency_options.scope: Requires AI to read and follow
